methods.name: Stage-Progression

methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Dexterity, a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Samir Menon and co-founders, is currently valued at $1.65B post-money after raising $291M to date. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of nine companies: one public comparable and eight private firms at similar stages, including 1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Collaborative Robotics, Fairmat, and GrayMatter Robotics.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchored to Dexterity's $1.6B last post-money valuation, applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B-to-next-stage advancement, implying $3.1B. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a $500M median, though the peer set exhibits substantial dispersion, ranging from $300M (GrayMatter Robotics) to $10.0B (1X Technologies), reflecting heterogeneity in robotics subsectors and maturity profiles.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $425M (low) to $3.6B (high), with a median of $1.8B. Dexterity's current $1.65B valuation aligns closely with this median, positioning the company near the middle of the derived range. Key uncertainties include the substantial gap between peer-median ($500M) and stage-progression results ($3.1B), driven by outlier valuations within the robotics cohort, and the limited public comparable data available to anchor sector benchmarking.
